element_introduction_order.mastery_context: lateral movement left/right control established immediately
acceleration and deceleration via joystick before combat pressure
button press available from beginning, no delay before first enemies
spatial boundaries define playable area; mirrored islands in center create tunnel vision
dual-action objects: fly over to refuel or destroy for points; fuel gauge pressure increases gameplay tension
stationary or slowly moving targets establish basic combat
faster/more mobile enemy type; Atari 8-bit version has helicopters that fire back
destructible checkpoint structures that segment the river; destroying a bridge creates a spawn restart point
as river progresses, fewer fuel tanks force player to manage risk/reward of destruction vs. refueling

typical_relief_triggers: successfully flying over a fuel depot to refill gauge
destroying a bridge and establishing new checkpoint
clearing a section of enemies without collision
reaching a fuel-rich early section after fuel scarcity
typical_tension_triggers: fuel gauge dropping visually toward empty
approaching river banks while maneuvering laterally
fuel depot scarcity as progression increases
klaxon-styled warning sound when fuel runs low
dense enemy cluster forcing evasion and collision avoidance
approaching a bridge that must be destroyed to progress
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating fuel pressure creates rising tension curve; infinite scrolling river provides no end-state relief, forcing sustained attention; early game offers fuel abundance and gentle pacing, mid-to-late game creates scarcity-driven decision pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: fuel depots and enemy clusters vary in frequency and positioning; destroying optional fuel depots trades immediate points for future fuel risk

difficulty_curve.early_game: abundant fuel supply, sparse enemies, gentle pacing establishes jet control and shooting mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: fuel depots begin spacing further apart, enemy variety increases (tankers, helicopters), bridge checkpoints introduce tactical navigation
difficulty_curve.late_game: fuel scarcity forces aggressive risk-taking; fewer safe zones; helicopter density increases; Atari 8-bit version adds return fire hazard
notable_spikes: transition from fuel abundance to scarcity creates psychological tension spike
first encounter with bridges introduces checkpoint-dependent navigation
late-game helicopter density and enemy clustering increase reaction demands

notable_level_patterns.description: river has no actual ending and scrolls infinitely downward (player perspective: forward); creates endless progression without win-state, only score maximization and survival duration
bridges serve as both destructible objectives and checkpoint restart points; destroying a bridge resets player spawn location at that bridge, creating natural level divisions within continuous scroll
fuel depot positioning and frequency varies by progression; fuel depletion creates mounting time pressure and forces tactical decisions (destroy for points vs. collect for survival)
river design mirrors left and right banks with island in center; creates tunnel-like navigation corridor and visual centering cue; based on Shaw's graph paper doodling approach
